body:
### Problem

Neovim crashes with a glibc heap corruption error (`corrupted size vs. prev_size`) when a **Markdown hover window** (triggered by `vim.lsp.buf.hover`) contains a Haskell code block and **tree-sitter-haskell** is installed. The crash occurs after closing the float window.

The problem is reproducible with the minimal configuration below and does **not** occur when tree-sitter-haskell is absent, nor with other language servers (e.g. Rust Analyzer).

### Steps to reproduce

### Steps to reproduce

1. Ensure `nvim-treesitter` is installed and the **haskell** parser is available.
   In Neovim:

   ```
   :TSInstall haskell
   ```
2. Create a minimal `minimal.lua` (see below).
3. Create a Haskell file `test.hs`:
   ```haskell
   main = do
     let s = "Hello\nworld"
     let sl = lines s
     return ()
   ```
4. Start Neovim with:
   ```bash
   nvim --clean -u minimal.lua test.hs
   ```
5. Place the cursor on the function `lines`.
6. Loop "press `K` and move cursor" for several times (first hover works, second hover shows different content).
7. Press `l` or any key to move cursor → **Neovim immediately crashes** and the terminal displays:
   `corrupted size vs. prev_size`
8. If it hasn't crashed yet, run `:restart` and repeat steps 5 to 7

### Minimal `minimal.lua`

```lua
-- Run this file as `nvim --clean -u minimal.lua`

for name, url in pairs {
  nvim_treesitter = 'https://github.com/nvim-treesitter/nvim-treesitter',
} do
  local install_path = vim.fn.fnamemodify('nvim_issue/' .. name, ':p')
  if vim.fn.isdirectory(install_path) == 0 then
    vim.fn.system { 'git', 'clone', '--depth=1', url, install_path }
  end
  vim.opt.runtimepath:append(install_path)
end

vim.cmd('runtime! plugin/nvim-treesitter.lua')

require('nvim-treesitter').setup {
  install_dir = vim.fn.stdpath('data') .. '/site'
}
require('nvim-treesitter').install { 'haskell' }

vim.lsp.config["hls"] = {
  cmd = { "haskell-language-server-wrapper", "--lsp" }
}
vim.lsp.enable("hls")
vim.keymap.set("n", "K", vim.lsp.buf.hover, {})

```

*(No other plugins, no custom autocmds, no additional options.)*
Haskell Language Server: `2.13.0.0` (GHC 9.6.7)

### Expected behavior

Hover window appears normally, no crash.

### Nvim version (nvim -v)

NVIM v0.13.0-dev-676+gcc056d78fb

### Vim (not Nvim) behaves the same?

no

### Operating system/version

WSL2 (Arch Linux, kernel 6.6.87.2-microsoft-standard-WSL2) , Windows 24H2

### Terminal name/version

WSL2 (Arch Linux, kernel 6.6.87.2-microsoft-standard-WSL2) , Windows 24H2

### $TERM environment variable

tmux-256color

### Installation

bob use nightly

comment_data.body: Cannot reproduce. Can you be more specific about what "press `K` and move cursor" refers to? Where did you move the cursor after pressing `K`, and how did you get a second hover that shows different content?

After starting the program and after the plugin has been loaded successfully, when I move the cursor to `lines` and press `K` (mapped to `vim.lsp.buf.hover()`), the hover window shows the following content:

```
lines :: String -> [String]

*Defined in ‘Data.OldList’* *(base-4.18.3.0)*
```

After moving the cursor so that the hover window disappears, pressing `K` again changes the content of the hover window to (sometimes it crashes at this step as well):

```
lines :: String -> [String]

*Defined in ‘Data.OldList’* *(base-4.18.3.0)*

Splits the argument into a list of **lines** stripped of their terminating  
`\n` characters. The `\n` terminator is optional in a final non-empty  
line of the argument string.

For example:  

>>> lines ""           -- empty input contains no lines
[]

>>> lines "\n"         -- single empty line
[""]

>>> lines "one"        -- single unterminated line
["one"]

>>> lines "one\n"      -- single non-empty line
["one"]

>>> lines "one\n\n"    -- second line is empty
["one",""]

>>> lines "one\ntwo"   -- second line is unterminated
["one","two"]

>>> lines "one\ntwo\n" -- two non-empty lines
["one","two"]

When the argument string is empty, or ends in a `\n` character, it can be  
recovered by passing the result of `lines` to the `unlines` function.  
Otherwise, `unlines` appends the missing terminating `\n`. This makes  
`unlines . lines` **idempotent**:

(unlines . lines) . (unlines . lines) = (unlines . lines)
```

When I then move the cursor to dismiss this hover window, Neovim may crash immediately; if it doesn't, repeatedly opening and closing the hover window will eventually trigger the crash.

The following is the backtrace viewed by gdb:

#76 0x00005567bc760be2 in state_enter (s=0x7ffed5fde8b0) at /home/runner/work/neovim/neovim/src/nvim/state.c:100

#78 0x00005567bc4963a2 in main (argc=<optimized out>, argv=<optimized out>) at /home/runner/work/neovim/neovim/src/nvim/main.c:687
```

This is likely an old issue with the haskell grammar/tree-sitter core, see https://github.com/tree-sitter/tree-sitter-haskell/issues/144. Merges to the tree-sitter org's copy of tree-sitter-haskell were blocked, so there is an updated version in the tree-sitter-grammars org here: https://github.com/tree-sitter-grammars/tree-sitter-haskell. Can you confirm which version of the parser you have and what version of tree-sitter-cli was used to generate it?

> When I then move the cursor to dismiss this hover window

Again, where did you move the cursor to dismiss the hover window?

I've taken several screenshots to demonstrate how I reproduce the issue: 1. This is the structure of my reproduction project. After running `:checkhealth nvim-treesitter`, the output is as follows: ```markdown nvim-treesitter: ✅ Requirements ~ - ✅ OK Neovim was compiled with tree-sitter runtime ABI version 15 (required >=13). - ✅ OK tree-sitter-cli 0.26.9 (/home/ely/.local/bin/tree-sitter) - ✅ OK tar 1.35.0 (/usr/sbin/tar) - ✅ OK curl 8.20.0 (/usr/sbin/curl) curl 8.20.0 (x86_64-pc-linux-gnu) libcurl/8.20.0 OpenSSL/3.6.3 zlib/1.3.2 brotli/1.2.0 zstd/1.5.7 libidn2/2.3.8 libpsl/0.21.5 libssh2/1.11.1 nghttp2/1.69.0 ngtcp2/1.23.0 nghttp3/1.16.0 mit-krb5/1.21.3 Release-Date: 2026-04-29 Protocols: dict file ftp ftps gopher gophers http https imap imaps ipfs ipns mqtt mqtts pop3 pop3s rtsp scp sftp smtp smtps telnet tftp ws wss Features: alt-svc AsynchDNS brotli GSS-API HSTS HTTP2 HTTP3 HTTPS-proxy IDN IPv6 Kerberos Largefile libz PSL SPNEGO SSL threadsafe TLS-SRP UnixSockets zstd OS Info ~ - machine: x86_64 - sysname: Linux - version: #1 SMP PREEMPT_DYNAMIC Thu Jun 5 18:30:46 UTC 2025 - release: 6.6.87.2-microsoft-standard-WSL2 Install directory for parsers and queries ~ - /home/ely/.local/share/nvim/site - ✅ OK is writable. - ✅ OK is in runtimepath. Installed languages H L F I J ~ - haskell ✓ ✓ ✓ . ✓ Legend: [H]ighlights, [L]ocals, [F]olds, [I]ndents, In[J]ections ~ ``` 2. After starting Neovim, I move the cursor to `lines`. 3. Run `:lua vim.lsp.buf.hover()` (i.e., what the `K` key is mapped to). A hover window appears showing the information seen in screenshot **3**. 4. Press `l` to move the cursor and dismiss the hover window. 5. Run `:lua vim.lsp.buf.hover()` again on `lines`. The hover window reappears, but its content is not exactly the same as the first time. 6. Now pressing `l` to dismiss the hover window may cause Neovim to crash immediately. If it does not crash at this point, repeating step **5** can trigger the crash.
Ah, so my understanding is right. However in ⑤ I get the same hover window as ③ instead of a larger hover window, so I still cannot reproduce this issue.

Similar issue, but occurs more frequently. Some times `corrupted size vs. prev_size` happens once I open a buffer with haskell file. This issue has only started occurring in the last few days. Previously, it was not this frequent.

@kalium222 What system did you encounter this issue on? I found that I couldn't reproduce it on another machine running Linux Mint either, so I'm guessing it might be related to Arch Linux?

Additionally, I sometimes also get the following error:

```
malloc(): mismatching next->prev_size (unsorted)
```

> Ah, so my understanding is right. However in ⑤ I get the same hover window as ③ instead of a larger hover window, so I still cannot reproduce this issue.

@zeertzjq do you have `haskell-language-server` set? If the lsp is not activated, this crash won't happen

[small nix env](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/28847151/reproduce.zip)
if somebody use `nix`, you may try above flake to reproduce. but the neovim binary the crashed is the arch one. 
I did not reproduce it on nixos one
[video](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/f090e423-6dbc-4bdc-8928-3dc6ab4b342a)
didn't cut the beginning, skip to 1:00

I find that I can reproduce without treesitter, only
```
vim.lsp.config['hls'] = {
  cmd = { 'haskell-language-server-wrapper', '--lsp' },
}
vim.lsp.enable('hls')
```
in `init.lua`. But with `$ nvim -u init.lua Main.hs` command. I cann't get crash with `--clean` flag

I have a repro in https://github.com/ejconlon/nvim-ts-hs-repro

UPDATE: Fails on 0.12.2 AND 0.12.3

The repro should be fixed if you pull the latest [commit](https://github.com/tree-sitter-grammars/tree-sitter-haskell/commit/98aedbd2d6947a168ba3ba3755d70b0cb6b78395) from tree-sitter-haskell. The grammar's external scanner was corrupting the heap due to strict aliasing violations in tree-sitter's provided `array.h` header.

> I find that I can reproduce without treesitter, only
> 
> ```
> vim.lsp.config['hls'] = {
>   cmd = { 'haskell-language-server-wrapper', '--lsp' },
> }
> vim.lsp.enable('hls')
> ```
> 
> in `init.lua`. But with `$ nvim -u init.lua Main.hs` command. I cann't get crash with `--clean` flag

title: Heap corruption (corrupted size vs. prev_size) when tree-sitter-haskell highlights a Markdown code block in hover float
